title: DocMed Landing Page (app/page.tsx)
content:
This file serves as the main landing page for the DocMed application. It integrates various UI components to present the product's features, benefits, and call-to-actions.

## Purpose

The primary purpose of this file is to render the marketing landing page for DocMed, engaging potential users with information about its self-healing documentation capabilities.

## Key Components and Exports

- **`Navbar`**: A responsive navigation bar with logo, navigation links, and sign-in/sign-up buttons. It includes a mobile-friendly menu.
- **`Hero`**: The prominent hero section featuring a dynamic globe animation, a compelling headline, a brief description, and primary call-to-action buttons.
- **`TrustBar`**: A section displaying key technology integrations and features as trust signals.
- **`Features`**: A section detailing the core features of DocMed, each with an icon, title, description, and a highlighted benefit.
- **`HowItWorks`**: Explains the three-step process of using DocMed, from integration to automated documentation maintenance.
- **`Stats`**: Presents key statistics and metrics related to DocMed's effectiveness.
- **`CTA` (Call to Action)**: A prominent section encouraging users to sign up, featuring an email input and a "Get Early Access" button.
- **`Footer`**: The website footer containing the logo and copyright information.
- **`Home` (default export)**: The main React component that orchestrates the rendering of all the above sections to form the complete landing page.

## Usage Example

This file is the entry point for the root path ('/') of the application. When a user navigates to the application's base URL, this `Home` component is rendered, displaying the full landing page experience.

```tsx
// In your Next.js application, navigating to the root URL ('/') will render this component.

// Example of how the main component is structured:
<main>
  <Navbar />
  <Hero />
  <TrustBar />
  <Features />
  <HowItWorks />
  <Stats />
  <CTA />
  <Footer />
</main>
```
